title: Xenial Spiced Moroccan lamb Tagine
description: A herbal and molten Moroccan dish featuring black treacle and shio-koji-cure, this tagine is a nostalgic and shojin-friendly delight, slow cooked to perfection in a traditional clay pot.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the black treacle and shio-koji, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees for 2.5 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
